item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the explanation that dr wondertainment’s creepy scp where just at a different time. (Probably the Cornelius area)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also click on 'options' at the bottom of the page then 'page source'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wondertainment does have its share of not-so-nice anomalies, some going far back in time. The Little Misters are a sad bunch, and sometimes people do got hurt.

I'm one who also like a lighthearted Wondertainment, but it isn't quite new to have a skip from there that was really hurt in the process of becoming what it is now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although I do agree with some of your critiques, I think some explanations are worth to be placed here.

1- It isn't treated like crap by the Foundation. Not many details are given for how it is being treated by the Foundation, except that it have a 800m² enclosure (reasonable for captivity standards and probably WWS wouldn't have something larger), a diet based on fruits and bamboo and a personal veterinarian.

2- The Foundation takes it away for very clear reasons, and though I do agree that they went more paranoid than I would have it, it isn't truly a lack of reason. Foundation and the numerous GOIs had a lot of bad blood between them in the past.

3- We had indeed more brazen and aggressive GOIs attacking Wondertainment anomalies before, like SCP-629 being taken from the CotBG.

4- Also there is a series of tales that backs up most of this interpretation of Wondertainment. The tale Miss Heir introduces Dr. Isabel Helga Anastasia Parvati Wondertainment V taking the mantle after the retirement of the previous Wondertainment, as a continuation of the tale SCP-REDD, that offers one of the best known closures to the Little Misters line (that defined a lot of early Wondertainment). H.L. can reasonably be a more sound of mind member of the Wondertainment thing, and it doesn't need to conform to linear time, so other interpretations of W can exist simultaneously.

5- On the same page, several SCPs and tales already gave Wondertainment a lot of knowledge about secret stuff, including the inner works of the Foundation, and the ability to deliver whatever they want to whomever they want, as in SCP-3301, a boardgame about the Foundation, delivered directly to its current containment site with a note addressed to the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can hit Edit on the page just to see the code and copy it, you know?

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
There's actually a tale about it here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/the-mon-key-to-happiness

I can't believe you missed it it's like your entire job

:P

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

This article already has a decent number of crosslinks, but I feel that it could use a few more.

First off: As this is a part of the What a Wonderful World canon, I suggest that the parent page of this piece be set to that page.

Secondly, there are two GoIs mentioned in this SCP, both of which could be linked as follows, at their earliest mention:

Special Containment Procedures: Full containment is deferred to GoI-466 (Wilson's Wildlife Solutions) as per the Boring Agreement1.

And

An investigation into a possible connection between SCP-3879 and Dr. Wondertainment was initiated.

Finally, there is one final crosslink of more substantial weight that the others which I think could be made, which is to add a link to the author's own tale, "I Don't Get It, But I'll Figure It Out.", which introduces Fae Wilson and her relationship with her father. Since this article has Tim Wilson mention Fae, an easy crosslink can be added there.

I'm going to include Fae's notes on Walter as an attachment. As his primary caretaker, she knew him better than anyone. I hope they'll be of use to you, but I also hope you folks reconsider and let us have Walter back. If there's something we're proud of here at Wilson's it's our ability to change a critter's life for the best

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

Footnotes
1. Following the Ursus Maritimus Incident of 2008, the Boring Agreement was created as a binding document which allows Wilson's Wildlife Solutions to handle the containment of Euclid or Safe fauna based anomalies within Clackamas County under supervision of the SCP Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This had me at the Wondertainment note. Simply wonderful. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels like this article is… not quite missing something, but that there's not much to it. There isn't that push to make me think "This is a good article" or "I'm glad I got to read this" or "This is hooking me in". to It's as well written as any good article can be expected to be, but it doesn't do anything for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is (to me) a prime example of storytelling in SCP documents. You understand all of Walter's history without going into an exposition dump, you learn of the different Wondertainment CEOs treatment of their products, and you get the Foundation's track-covering between emails and GoI conspirators.

My only regret is that I didn't read this sooner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm quite conflicted with this article's canon. I always viewed Dr. Wondertainment ran by one guy who controls most of his business, but not exactly. Despite things, all his creations are wonderful and no neglect or abuse come to them. I mean, the modifications sound like the work of The Factory. Besides, I like to think Dr. Wondertainment is unique.

Another thing is that is the Wilson's Wildlife Solutions. I was always a bit iffy about the concept of the group for I feel what they have isn't much of a problem for the Foundation to keep. Maybe I lack some knowledge of the group, but it's an eh.

Because I like the anomaly, I will up vote it and maybe come up with a way to incorporate it into my canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Crit
 

Okay so we've got WWS somehow getting a gorilla from wondertainment that the foundation took away and treated like crap…for some reason. You never clearly explain why the foundation takes it away. This whole series of events is nonsensical outside of having making a plot which drastically cheapens the emotional value. Basically the foundation becomes a villain without a motive here. I assume from the last email that they're trying to protect it from people that apparently want to damage/kill wondertainment items but it's only brought up there and might be a lie anyway.
More specifically:

An investigation into a possible connection between SCP-3879 and Dr. Wondertainment was initiated.

Ok so it was with WWS for so long because the foundation took a while to get around to investigating it. Did nobody in an office somewhere see "wondertainment" and "W"? Why is WWS such a low priority anyway? You make it very clear how important they are to preserve earth's ecosystem and humanity.

I hope he may be able to lead as normal a life as he can under your care and supervision. Once again, thank you.

A very crucial part of wondertainment is it not being clear what it actually is. You make it pretty apparent that it's not only run by people that can be over-thrown but has staff that can communicate sanely with empathy and normal people emotions, one of which directly communicates with wilson…somehow. And they apparently already knew one another? It'd be different if WWS was weird and spooky too but they have contact with the foundation and go along with them, But never told the foundation anything about a connection to wondertainment and might even know one of the leaders' names. There's a pretty big dissonance with trust there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a question? How did you do the wondertainmente notes ?
Manly the wondertainmente letter .

I'm working on scp ,and it have a letter in it.
I think I'll help overall with the scp and its stly ,I guess . Because the letter gave hint to its creation and why .

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally some background for WWS!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lots of musical animals at WWS, huh? I love the Wondertainment/WWS interaction and found myself feeling extremely sorry for poor Walter. Hope the Foundation treats Walter well!!

I don't necessarily agree with comments that the emotional content of the skip is neutralized by the fact that the Foundation has done and continues to do much worse things — if anything, I'd argue that that might actually make the situation worse. I think that the meaninglessness of Walter's tragedy in the greater scheme of things is rather sad in itself.

+1 from me. Love you and your work, Nico!! <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Walter, what a dear,
plays lovely tunes for our ears.
Let him be happy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The apparent kicker of this SCP is the Foundation doing something pragmatic but potentially bad for the anomaly itself.

To someone not used to this sort of thing, this may seem tragic. But this is also the Foundation, who does many, many, worse things in the course of normal business. Whatever emotional content the article hopes to convey is just neutered by that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm still of the opinion that using the gorilla as a "vechile" for the GoI interaction somewhat hurts the skip; I think the incidents that the skip was directly involved in could use a bit more fleshing out. Nevertheless, this is a good skip.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You rock uncle

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ok so basically im monky

+1

Also big love for the mention of Genghis!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
still good after all that reading and not giving crits (sorry)

semi-large +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i wish i could upvote comments

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's a little thing I made for you Nico…

GoIball Incoming
GoIball Delivered

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<3 <3 <3

My GoI and my favorite other GoI, the two GoIs that I write for, meet? And then it's my canon of the latter GoI?? Upvote central, my dude. Love Walter, and I hope that the Foundation takes care of him well enough. If the Foundation is lenient enough to work with WWS, then they may be caring enough to give Walter reasonable accommodations. That's the hope, anyways.

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
A lot of people to thank for this one so let's go through 'em real quick.

9Volt, tretter, JanitorCakeworth, DrAnnoyingDog Gabriel Jade, Eskobar, DarkStuff, not_a_seagull, MaliceAforethought and… I think I'm missing people still. If you read this, thank you all.

So in case you didn't notice, there are two Wondertainments in this article. The more S1-ish cold, uncaring capitalist that makes weird and sometimes slightly macabre toys and the wonderful Vend-A-Friend Wondertainment, who you can read more about here.

In case you can't tell, I really hate bureaucracy.

If that ending was too bittersweet for you, don't worry. I've got something for you. Just don't tell the Foundation. ;)
